parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 cumulative diff adds 6600+ lines including a new retry/backoff subsystem (retry.mjs) and BigModel error classifier (bigmodel-errors.mjs), but ships without UAT evidence for v0.4.6 — the exact release that introduced these critical-path modules. A library module (process.mjs) was deleted without visible confirmation that all import sites were migrated. The retry/error-classification coupling is the most impactful behavioral change in the entire diff and has only unit-test evidence, no end-to-end UAT under failure conditions. Block until v0.4.6 UAT is produced and import safety after process.mjs removal is confirmed.

findings.title: Missing UAT validation for v0.4.6 retry/backoff feature
findings.body: UAT reports exist for v0.4.4 and v0.4.5 but not for v0.4.6 (HEAD, tagged). The v0.4.6 release added BigModel error dispatch (bigmodel-errors.mjs, 168 lines) and auto retry/backoff (retry.mjs, 203 lines) — the most impactful runtime behavior change in the entire cumulative diff. These modules control whether API calls succeed, retry, or silently fail. Unit tests exist (retry.test.mjs: 352 lines, bigmodel-errors.test.mjs: 189 lines) but cover isolated logic, not the integrated glm-client → error-classify → retry flow under real failure conditions (timeouts, rate limits, unexpected error codes). Shipping v0.4.7 cumulative without this evidence means the highest-risk behavioral change is unvalidated end-to-end.
findings.recommendation: Produce a v0.4.6 UAT report covering: (1) successful retry on transient 5xx errors, (2) backoff timing under repeated failures, (3) correct terminal failure after max retries with user-visible error, (4) error classification for all documented BigModel error codes, (5) behavior on unrecognized/unexpected error codes. Do not ship v0.4.7 cumulative without this evidence.

findings.title: Deleted process.mjs — unverified complete import migration
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines) was removed as part of 'Bundle F — ReferenceError + full thread/resume scaffolding purge'. If any remaining runtime path imports from this module, it will throw MODULE_NOT_FOUND. Multiple core modules were modified in the same period (glm-companion.mjs: 139 lines changed, render.mjs: 79 lines changed, state.mjs: 32 lines changed), but the diff stat alone cannot confirm every import site was updated. The commit message referencing 'ReferenceError' in the deletion commit suggests the removal itself was a reactive fix, increasing the risk the migration was not systematic.
findings.recommendation: Run `grep -rn 'process\.mjs\|from.*process' scripts/` against the current tree to confirm no live import references remain. Add a CI gate that validates all static import targets resolve at lint time. If any reference is found, this is a runtime crash.

findings.title: Retry/error-classification coupling creates silent misclassification surface
findings.body: All BigModel API call retry behavior now depends on correct classification by bigmodel-errors.mjs. If an error type is unrecognized or misclassified, the consequences are asymmetric: (1) a non-retryable error retried wastes API quota and adds latency; (2) a retryable error failing immediately causes user-visible failures that should have self-healed. The 168-line error classifier has unit tests, but external API error taxonomies change without notice. There is no visible structured logging at the retry decision point that would surface a misclassification in production — retries either happen silently or don't, with no diagnostic trail. An unrecognized error code that falls through to a default retry path could mask a permanent failure as transient.
findings.recommendation: Add structured logging at the retry decision point emitting: original error code/status, classified retryability, attempt number, and backoff delay. Implement a fail-safe default for unrecognized errors: log at warn level and default to NO retry (so permanent failures surface immediately rather than silently burning retry budget). Add a CI test asserting every documented BigModel error code has an explicit classification entry.

next_steps: Grep the current tree for any remaining imports of scripts/lib/process.mjs — if found, block on the resulting runtime crash bug
Produce v0.4.6 UAT report exercising retry/backoff end-to-end with simulated API failures before cutting v0.4.7
Verify bigmodel-errors.mjs handles unknown/unexpected error codes with a safe no-retry default and warn-level logging
Add a CI smoke test validating the full glm-client → error-classify → retry pipeline resolves without unhandled exceptions on all paths
Consider adding an integration-level test in tests/result-propagation.test.mjs that exercises the retry + error-classify coupling with mock network failures
